# bitchat

> On-device voice chat CLI for macOS 26+ on Apple Silicon. ASR + LLM + TTS
> all run locally via mlx-swift; an optional video mode adds a lip-synced
> animated avatar via the bitHuman expression engine. No network calls, no
> API keys. Built and maintained by bitHuman (https://www.bithuman.ai).

`bitchat` is a Homebrew-installable command-line tool. A user running it
can have a real-time spoken conversation with a local LLM. Three modes:
text-only chat, audio-only voice chat, and video chat (voice + a small
floating avatar window with lip-synced face animation). Designed to feel
like cloud voice assistants but stay 100% on-device.

## Install

```
brew tap bithuman-product/bitchat
brew install bitchat
```

Requires macOS 26 (Tahoe)+, Apple Silicon (M1+), ≥ 8 GB RAM (16 GB
recommended for video mode), ~3 GB free disk for voice mode and ~7 GB for
video mode (cached at `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/` and
`~/Library/Application Support/bithuman/` after the first launch).

## Run

```
bitchat                                # voice (default) — audio chat
bitchat text                           # text-only chat
bitchat video                          # voice + animated avatar
bitchat video --image ~/me.jpg         # video chat with a portrait

# common flags (work across modes when applicable)
bitchat --locale ja-JP                 # Japanese ASR + TTS
bitchat --voice Aiden                  # English preset speaker (voice mode)
bitchat --voice ~/voices/me.wav        # clone a voice from a 10–20 s clip
bitchat --prompt "Be terse."           # override system prompt
bitchat --prompt @/path/to/persona.txt # … or read it from a file
bitchat --help                         # full usage
```

Voice-mode TTS preset speakers (Qwen3-TTS): English (`Ryan`, `Aiden`);
Chinese (`Vivian`, `Serena`, `Uncle_Fu`, `Dylan`, `Eric`).

Video-mode TTS voices (Kokoro): `af_heart`, `af_alloy`, `af_aoede`,
`af_kore`, `bf_emma`, `am_adam`, `am_michael`, `am_echo`, `bm_george`.

Cancel the bot's reply at any point by speaking — barge-in cutoff is under
80 ms end-to-end (audio + avatar both, in video mode). Quit with Cmd-Q,
Ctrl-C, or right-click → "Quit bitchat".

## Video mode

`bitchat video` opens a small floating circular window with a talking face.
The avatar's lip movements stay frame-paired with the bot's audio at
25 FPS via the bitHuman expression engine (Wav2Vec2 → DiT → VAE).

A colored ring around the avatar signals state: cyan = listening,
violet = thinking, amber = speaking. A label below echoes the same.

Idle GPU usage is near-zero: bitchat captures ~10 s of idle DiT
frames into a palindrome cache, then loops them forward / reverse
forever instead of regenerating. The cache invalidates when the
portrait changes (drag-drop, agent pick, --image swap), then
refills over the next ~10 s. Speech still streams through DiT live.

Right-click the avatar to customize:

- **Choose agent…** — gallery of 8 bundled personas (face + voice +
  prompt as a coherent set). Names: Diego (default), Nova, Einstein,
  Riya, Lena, Rae, Dr. Maya, Mason. Picking a card swaps all three.
- **Change image…** — pick a portrait file or drag-drop one onto the
  avatar. ~5 s VAE encode then the avatar speaks through your portrait.
- **Change voice…** — gallery of 9 Kokoro voices grouped Feminine /
  Masculine. Click to audition, Save to commit.
- **Change prompt…** — multi-line editor with 6 starter templates
  (Companion, Coach, Tutor, Storyteller, Coding buddy, Calm listener).

## Architecture (high-level)

| layer | implementation |
|---|---|
| ASR | Apple SpeechAnalyzer (`Speech` framework, on-device, macOS 26+) |
| LLM | `mlx-community/gemma-4-E2B-it-4bit` via `ml-explore/mlx-swift-lm` |
| TTS — voice mode | `mlx-community/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base-4bit` via `Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift` (voice cloning supported) |
| TTS — video mode | `mlx-community/Kokoro-82M-4bit` via `Blaizzy/mlx-audio-swift` (preset voices, lighter weight for A/V sync) |
| Avatar — video mode | bitHuman expression engine: Wav2Vec2 audio encoder + DiT denoiser + VAE encoder |
| AEC | hardware: `AVAudioEngine` voice-processing IO unit, both ends |
| A/V sync — video mode | chunk-paired playback: each TTS audio chunk + the matching DiT video frames are queued together at 25 FPS |
| Voice cloning — voice mode | in-context speaker conditioning via Qwen3-TTS `refAudio` + `refText` |

Source code (Swift 6, SPM) lives in a private dev repo
(`bithuman-product/bitchat-dev`). External installs come through this
public repo's Homebrew formula and notarised release zips.

## Library use (Swift)

Embedding bitchat as a library (`BitChatKit` SwiftPM target) is supported
but currently invitation-only. The public API surface looks like:

```swift
import BitChatKit

var config = VoiceChatConfig()
config.localeIdentifier = "en-US"
config.systemPrompt = "You are a deadpan ship's computer. One sentence."
config.voice = .preset("Aiden")
// or .clone(referenceAudio: URL, transcript: String)
// or .default

// Optional avatar config for video mode:
// config.avatar = AvatarConfig(modelPath: weightsURL, portraitPath: portraitURL)

let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
try await chat.start()

// Curated agent library:
for agent in AgentCatalog.all { ... }
```

Open an issue at https://github.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bitchat/issues
if you'd like access to the source repo for library embedding.

Apache-2.0 for the Swift source and the bundled Gemma / Kokoro / Qwen3-TTS
weights. The bitHuman expression-engine SDK ships under the bitHuman SDK
license; see LICENSE in the dev repo for details.

bitHuman Inc. — https://www.bithuman.ai.
